channel.title: Software Engineering on Home
channel.description: Recent content in Software Engineering on Home

item.title: Designing Secure Authentication Systems
item.description: Architecture and operational guidance for building authentication systems that limit damage, detect compromise, and recover cleanly.

item.title: Legacy System Insights
item.description: A practical look at legacy systems through the lenses of incentives, trust, incremental change, rewrite risk, and observability.

item.title: Modern Mobile Hardening
item.description: A lifecycle-based model for mobile security that treats the client as hostile and keeps trust anchored in backend policy.

item.title: AI SDLC: automating the grind
item.description: Why software delivery feels like a grind loop and how AI can help teams preserve context across the SDLC.

item.title: Software Architecture for Independence
item.description: A short pointer to a longer essay on architectural independence, AI, and security tradeoffs.

item.title: Eyespie (formerly Scouter)
item.description: A prototype image-based social game that evolved through multiple rewrites toward an offline-first, ML-assisted MVP.

item.title: Guitar Suite Demos
item.description: An old demo reel from a cross-platform music app project built long before the current stack.

item.title: Git Notes Storage
item.description: Using git notes as a lightweight place to store build metadata without polluting the main repository history.

item.title: Fortunes on Slack
item.description: A small Go microservice that serves classic Unix fortunes through an HTTP API and a Slack slash command.

item.title: Elixir and Phoenix
item.description: An experiment building a lightweight chat app in Elixir and Phoenix to explore the Erlang VM.

item.title: Best Programming Quotes
item.description: A personal collection of programming quotes about craft, simplicity, bugs, and engineering reality.
